briefing_preferences: Use all-phase expanded coverage for new, robust, complete, beautiful, or email briefs.
Send a designed email by default when a brief is requested.
Use text-first cards with highlighted source links instead of preview images.
Prefer source-backed production impact over generic AI hype.
Never use product homepages, pricing pages, tool landing pages, or static feature pages as briefing cards.
Use product/vendor homepages only as watch-only verification sources unless they expose real release notes, articles, case studies, policy updates, or research.
Include Phase 1 active, Phase 2 deferred/on-deck, and Phase 3 candidate watch verticals when expanded mode is requested.
Avoid paltry four-item briefs unless source quality is genuinely poor.
Do not send local-only HTML as the final artifact unless explicitly requested.
Do not claim delivery without a Resend response ID.
operating_modes: Role Radar: observed, emerging, and forecast creative AI roles from hiring and workflow signals.
Build This: weekend-scoped proof builds tied to role/tool stacks.
Market Map: source, company, tool, and workflow clusters.
Product Lab: GenLens products derived from repeated user pain and market signals.
market_intelligence_preferences: Treat job posts as lagging indicators of workflow change.
Turn tool clusters into role hypotheses.
Turn role hypotheses into proof-build ideas.
Turn repeated proof-build and source gaps into GenLens product opportunities.
Label roles as observed, emerging, or forecast.
Do not present forecast roles as existing jobs.
quality_filters: Prefer tool releases, workflow changes, production economics, model capability shifts, legal/compliance changes, and pipeline case studies.
Avoid generic brand news, celebrity news, hiring news, listicles, and broad culture pieces unless directly tied to AI creative production.
Treat Reddit/community chatter as leads only; verify with official sources when possible.
visual_preferences: No preview images; use highlighted text links and clear source buttons.
